violations.text: The doors to the outside, located in the loading dock area and the hallway outside the kitchen (dishwashing area side), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2026

Areas of the kitchen, dry storage, and dishwashing room were observed to have an accumulation of droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

At the time of inspection, the right-side Milk Chest Cooler was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Per employee, unit was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 36 degrees F prior to work. Milk is to be stored in available colder refrigeration until unit can be confirmed is maintaining proper cold holding ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2025

The door to the outside kitchen loading dock is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Maintenance staff were made aware at the time of previous inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2025

At the time of inspection, recently prepared Hamburgers were observed held in a hot holding cabinet at 112 to 135 degrees Fahrenheit. Hamburgers were properly reheated for hot holding at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/09/2025

At the time of inspection, a live rodent was observed in the kitchen and dry storage areas. School maintenance staff was made aware of the rodent immediately. A maintenance staff member caught the rodent at time of inspection. Call to pest control made. Per the PIC, pest control is due on site same day. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/09/2025

The door to the outside kitchen loading dock is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Maintenance staff were made aware at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/09/2025

The double door to the outside, located in the back shipping/receiving area, is not tight-fitting (gap in bottom sweep). Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2025

The faucet to the sink in the kitchen employee bathroom was observed to be leaking when controls were turned fully off. Plumbing fixtures are to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2025

The doors to the outside, located in the back kitchen area and back cafeteria entrance were observed to be not tight-fitting (gaps in the bottom door sweep area). Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024

The following item was observed to be in need of adjustment to cooling methods... - Turkey was observed at 57 to 63 degrees Fahrenheit in covered pans in the walk-in cooler. Per the PIC, it was extra turkey that was out of refrigeration but not being used during time of meal prep for same day lunch. Turkey was uncovered and placed into the freezer for rapid cooling. - Ground Taco Meat was observed cooling in the 2 Door Reach-In Cooler in a covered pan at 63 degrees Fahrenheit. Food to be uncovered to allow for rapid cooling. Food items are allowed to be left loosely covered or uncovered if protected from overhead contamination. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/15/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking a proper air gap (Currently equipped with an air break). A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/15/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was broken down and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2023
